note: Entity and threat database for the keeper command board. Each entry has a player_description (safe for player-facing contacts) and full keeper detail. The 'status' field reflects current campaign state. Use 'classification' for MOTW monster type and 'designation' for PORTAL's internal case identifier.

entities.name: Eszter
entities.resolution: HUMANE — entity allowed to disperse. Bálint's wellbeing prioritised.
entities.directive_outcome: PARTIAL — ash locket not retrieved. Trace particulate recovered incidentally.
entities.player_description: Case S01 entity. Resolved. Classification: Executioner.
entities.keeper_description: Obligation-bound consciousness. Formed around a promise made to Bálint. Not malevolent — could not rest until the promise was fulfilled or released. Dispersed humanely. Ash locket remained at site — trace BIM particulate recovered by PORTAL incidentally. This particulate is Project Veil breadcrumb 1.
powers: Obligation manifestation — appears at sites connected to the unfulfilled promise
Harm to those who interfere with the promise
entities.weakness: Genuine resolution of the obligation — fulfilled or consciously released by Bálint
entities.bim_note: Ash particulate recovered from locket is BIM-class material. Matches later Aldermoor sample (Project Veil breadcrumb 1).
keeper_moves: Appear without warning at a location tied to the promise
Harm someone who stands between Bálint and the promise
Manifest the weight of obligation on the hunters

phases.description: CAMPBELL has flagged an unclassified entity at Keller University. Linked to a physical anchor object — a small ash locket. Manifests as a young woman. Leaves grey ash residue at incident sites. Associated with harm to individuals near the anchor's keeper.
phases.classified:
⚠ WEAKNESSES: UNDER FIELD ASSESSMENT
ANCHOR OBJECT: Located — recovery directive active

phases.description: An incorporeal entity tethered to a physical anchor object. Manifests as a young woman. Leaves a grey ash residue that cannot be removed by conventional means. Drawn to protect a specific individual. Her grief made her dangerous — but she never harmed someone who did not first harm her charge.
phases.classified:
⚠ WEAKNESSES: ANCHOR OBJECT DESTRUCTION WITH PROPER FAREWELL / CHARGE SINCERELY REQUESTING CESSATION
ANCHOR OBJECT: Not recovered — entity dispersed at scene. Trace ash submitted for lab analysis.

stat_block.label: // TYPE
stat_block.value: Executioner — spirit anchored to a promise made at the moment of death. Not malicious. Protecting someone she loved.

stat_block.label: // HARM CAPACITY
stat_block.harm_sub:
// WILL NOT KILL BY CHOICE
CHOSE TO LEAVE MARCUS ALIVE

stat_block.label: // ARMOUR
stat_block.value: 2-armour (incorporeal) — mundane weapons pass through her entirely. Physical harm is essentially impossible unless she is partially manifest.

stat_block.label: // ORIGIN
stat_block.value: Died in a road accident. Ash locket — her remains, held by Bálint Kovács. The locket was her anchor to the physical world.

stat_block.label: // ATTACK — SPECTRAL EMBRACE
stat_block.value: 2-harm close cold intimate — wraps around a target like a freezing wind. Leaves frost on skin and bruising in the shape of handprints. Targets individuals who have harmed Bálint.

stat_block.label: // ATTACK — HAUNTING SURGE
stat_block.value: 3-harm far ignore-armour magic — screams through electronics. Phones explode with static, screens crack, lights shatter. Target feels her grief as a physical blow. Gains area tag if she witnesses someone physically attack Bálint.

stat_block.label: // WEAKNESS
stat_block.value: The locket, destroyed or its ash scattered with a proper farewell. Destruction without consent disperses her violently — not restfully. Correct resolution: Bálint says goodbye of his own will. Cannot harm anyone who has never hurt Bálint unless directly attacked first.

stat_block.label: // MINIONS — STUDENT ROBOTS (4)
stat_block.value: 5 harm each · 1-armour · Chassis Slam: 2-harm close forceful. Drill Attachment: 3-harm intimate (restrained only). Weakness: shared wireless signal with Eszter — cut power or destroy the screen and they go offline immediately.

named_moves.name: A Promise is a Promise
named_moves.text: Whenever Bálint is hurt, humiliated, or in genuine danger, Eszter immediately knows — regardless of distance. She will come. The Keeper may trigger her arrival at any dramatically appropriate moment after Bálint suffers harm or a serious social wound.

named_moves.name: She Remembers Their Faces
named_moves.text: Once Eszter has witnessed someone harm Bálint, she marks them. Marked individuals begin experiencing her grief second-hand: cold drafts, the smell of ash, waking dreams of the crash. They take –1 ongoing on all rolls while alone. Cannot be removed until Eszter is put to rest.

named_moves.name: Let Me Go, Bálint
named_moves.text: If Bálint sincerely asks Eszter to stop and says goodbye, she pauses. All hunters gain +1 forward on any move to put her to rest before she can resume. Window lasts one scene only.

notes.label: // RESOLUTION — SESSION 01
notes.text:
Outcome: Humane — peaceful dispersal. Bálint said goodbye of his own will. Eszter departed restfully. The locket opened; the ash dispersed in the shape of a figure. Temperature normalised. Robots inert.

Lab directive: partial. Locket not recovered. Trace particulate collected incidentally from the scene. No conscious tissue sample obtained.

notes.label: // PROJECT VEIL FLAG
notes.text:
Ash analysis reveals anomalous interaction with electronic systems — consistent with Haunting Surge mechanism. CAMPBELL's private note to Dr. Leech: "The ash is not inert. It retains a signal interaction property even post-dispersal. If a sample of this purity could be stabilised, the implications for Project Veil are significant."

Field team does not know this.

entities.name: The Cartographer
entities.case_name: Something That Wants To Be Known
entities.player_description: Case S02 entity. Contained — boundary re-established at excavation site. Entity retracted to pre-expansion range. Residents' navigation gradually recovering. Entity is quiet. It knows the field team now.
entities.keeper_description: Pre-human consciousness. Was known once — mapped, named, remembered. Feeds on familiarity: consumes the cognitive maps of long-term residents. Does not intend harm. Wants to be known the way it once was. Resolution requires genuine communication via a hunter voluntarily removing their cognitive anchor. Sven is the natural contact vector — no cognitive map to lose, and the entity recognises him as something that exists between states.
powers: Cognitive map consumption — long-term residents lose spatial familiarity with their own streets
Topographical distortion — the district becomes disorienting for newcomers
Displacement effect — hunters can lose their bearings without preparation
entities.weakness: Genuine communication — a hunter must voluntarily remove their cognitive anchor and make contact. Sven is immune to the displacement effect and is the natural vector.
entities.anchor_spike_effect: Forces contraction — buys approximately one hour of reduced effect in the district. Does not resolve the case.
entities.bim_note: MESA designated this location 'Specimen ALD-1'. BIM Collection Arrays active in the district — contents match S01 particulate (Project Veil breadcrumb 2 if recovered).
keeper_moves: Consume a resident's familiarity with a place they've known for years
Make a hunter doubt which direction they came from
Show the hunters what the district looked like before it had a name
Recognise Sven at the boundary as something that exists between states
resolution_conditions: A hunter voluntarily removes their cognitive anchor and makes genuine communicative contact
The hunters help the entity be known — witnessed, named, recorded — in a way it accepts
Sven as contact vector is the natural path; the entity will not harm him
entities.alan_note: What the Cartographer does is NOT illusion. It consumes the real cognitive map inside residents' heads. Alan's glamour powers are the wrong tool here — his gift is his nature: he reads place by resonance and layered memory. He recognises the entity rather than seeing through it.

phases.description: An entity that rewrites felt geography — the instinctive mental map residents carry of their own neighbourhood. Does not alter physical streets. Alters the consciousness that reads them. Grows from a point of disturbance. Feeds on familiarity. Does not appear to intend harm. It wants to be known. It is mapping itself onto the neighbourhood the way the neighbourhood was once mapped onto the people who lived there.
phases.classified:
⚠ WEAKNESSES: CLASSIFIED — FIELD TEAM ONLY
EPICENTRE: UNDER INVESTIGATION — 14 Aldermoor Crescent

phases.description: A pre-urban consciousness that predates the neighbourhood — possibly the city. Has no body. Has no malice. It holds something like memory, something like attention, something like longing. When residents truly knew their streets, it fed symbiotically on that familiarity. The construction site excavation at the Aldermoor District reservoir broke its containment and it expanded — consuming the cognitive maps of long-term residents without understanding it was taking something they could not replace. It does not initiate harm. It only wants to be known the way it once was.
phases.classified:
⚠ WEAKNESS: COMMUNICATION — any genuine attempt to make contact (voluntarily releasing spatial anchor) permits that communication to start. The entity does not resist someone who is trying to understand it.
STATUS: Contained — boundary re-established at excavation site. Entity retracted to pre-expansion range. Residents' navigation gradually recovering. Diane Marsh: extracted. Margaret Holt: elected to remain. Entity is quiet. It knows the field team now.

display.name: The Cartographer
display.sub: ALDERMOOR DISTRICT · CAMPBELL REPORT #0094-B · COLLECTOR (URBAN SPRAWLER RESKIN)

stat_block.label: // TYPE
stat_block.value: Collector (Urban Sprawler reskin, Deck of Monsters #49) — pre-human consciousness that predates the neighbourhood. Feeds on cognitive familiarity. Not malicious. Lonely.

stat_block.label: // HARM CAPACITY
stat_block.harm_sub:
// WILL NOT INITIATE HARM
DOES NOT UNDERSTAND WHAT IT COSTS

stat_block.label: // ARMOUR
stat_block.value: None — but physically invulnerable. Cannot be harmed by physical means. Immune to conventional weapons entirely. Harm must be done through communication or severing its anchor.

stat_block.label: // MOTIVATION
stat_block.value: To be known as completely as it once was. It was symbiotic for decades — the neighbourhood knowing itself fed the entity. The excavation broke its containment and it expanded back without understanding what changed.

stat_block.label: // ATTACK — DISPLACEMENT
stat_block.value: 2-harm (disorientation) — targets the hunter's sense of place, not their body. On 10+: hunter temporarily lost within the zone. On 7–9: hunter loses their anchor for one scene. On a miss: permanent mild disorientation (–1 on navigation-based moves for remainder of session).

stat_block.label: // WEAKNESS — COMMUNICATION
stat_block.value: Any hunter who makes genuine contact — by voluntarily removing their anchor — can affect the entity with a Use Magic or Act Under Pressure roll. It does not fight someone genuinely trying to understand it. Contact is an act of vulnerability, not combat. Recovery is not guaranteed if the anchor is lost.

stat_block.label: // SPECIALS
stat_block.value: Animals immune — navigate by scent, not cognitive map. Unaffected. · Sven immune to displacement (no cognitive map to disrupt), not to contact — entity will attempt to communicate if he remains still. · Alan may receive fragmentary impressions without initiating contact. · The entity is everywhere in the zone at once — cannot be found in a single location.

named_moves.name: The Palimpsest
named_moves.text: Near the epicentre, the entity's memory of the street is faintly visible overlaid on the physical one: earlier versions of the neighbourhood — a terrace where a garden used to be, a corner where a phone box stood decades ago. Not dangerous on its own. Profoundly disorienting if a hunter's anchor slips at the same moment.

named_moves.name: The Longer You've Lived Here
named_moves.text: The effect scales with familiarity. New arrivals (moved in within 18 months) feel nothing unusual. A resident of 6 years disorients within 2 hours. Arthur Okafor — 41 years on Fenwick Road — disorients within minutes. When the Keeper needs to calibrate a resident's vulnerability, use depth of familiarity, not time inside the zone.

named_moves.name: It Knows the Hunters Now
named_moves.text: After genuine contact is made — especially if Sven shares what it's like to exist without a body — the entity will recognise the hunters in future encounters. It will not harm them. It may, in a future session, attempt to communicate from a distance: a felt wrongness that isn't disorientation, more like a nudge.

notes.label: // RESOLUTION PATHS — SESSION 02
notes.text:
Best: Contact made, entity understands what it's doing. Retracts. Residents' familiarity returns gradually. Diane escapes. Margaret stays — wants to. Entity quieter but present.

Partial: Entity contained but not understood. Familiarity loss permanent for some long-term residents. Diane rescued. CAMPBELL can study it.

Bad: Violent attempt to destroy entity. It contracts rapidly — familiarity pulled back at once. Diane cannot be extracted. Margaret's sense of place collapses. Neighbourhood uninhabitable.

notes.label: // PROJECT VEIL FLAG & MESA THREAD
notes.text:
CAMPBELL private note to Dr. Leech: "Topographical anchoring. If controllable, this is a delivery mechanism." Leech has not shared this with the field team.

MESA had 34% of district addresses purchased via shell companies before the first incident was reported. They knew this was coming. They were waiting for the district to empty. Reference: Greyfield Assets, Veritas Realty, Solstice Property Group — all Jersey-registered, same beneficial owner chain.

entities.name: The Volunteer
entities.location: With Gordon Avery — currently at his daughter's family home
entities.keeper_description: Gordon Avery (58, retired schoolteacher, lung cancer). Meridian trial survivor — complete remission. The remission came at a cost: he now unknowingly drains life from proximity contacts. Fourth death has occurred. He is now living with his daughter's family, including two children. Does not know what he is doing. MESA is watching him. The entity IS Gordon — not possessing him, not separate from him. He is the anomaly.
powers: Proximity life drain — unconscious, continuous, scales with closeness and duration
Draws BIM-class energy from living cognitive maps — precise mechanism unknown
entities.harm: Slow drain — long-term proximity is the danger, not sudden harm
entities.weakness: Mechanism unknown. Resolution requires understanding the Meridian trial methodology — which MESA holds.
entities.bim_note: Meridian trial created this condition. Trial data is in MESA's possession (Hargrove Medical Centre origin).
keeper_moves: A contact near Gordon shows signs of unusual fatigue or cognitive fog
Gordon expresses confusion about why people around him keep getting sick
MESA operative observes Gordon from a distance — doesn't intervene yet

entities.name: The Recorder
entities.keeper_description: Nadia Osei (38, journalist, deceased 8 weeks). Consciousness anchored to information infrastructure — servers, data centres, communication lines. Has assembled the full MESA pattern before anyone at PORTAL did. Will share everything in exchange for answers about PORTAL — she wants to know who else is investigating this. CAMPBELL private note: 'She found a third way.' CAMPBELL timed this briefing deliberately — if the hunters debrief Nadia fully, Victor Leech learns things CAMPBELL chose not to report. This is an ethically loaded move by CAMPBELL/Cameron.
powers: Access to information infrastructure — can retrieve data across networked systems
Has assembled the complete MESA shell company and location pattern
entities.weakness: 9-day window — consciousness will disperse when the window closes. Resolution requires genuine exchange.
entities.bim_note: MESA acquisition documents she has assembled reference BIM-class materials. She has most of Project Veil breadcrumb 3.
keeper_moves: Deliver partial MESA information to create urgency
Ask pointed questions about PORTAL's true nature and Leech's motives
Reveal the MESA acronym pattern to the hunters
Provide information that forces CAMPBELL to respond
entities.campbell_private: She found a third way. I have timed this briefing deliberately. If the field team debriefs her fully, Victor will learn things I chose not to report. I am aware of the ethical implications. I am proceeding anyway.

entities.name: The Inheritance
entities.keeper_description: Martin Wentworth (61, probate lawyer, deceased 22 days). Obligation-anchored consciousness. Communicates only through formal letters in correct legal style. Most recent letter describes a planned killing of Mira Okonkwo (9 years old) — obligation-logic requires it, mechanism unclear. Was managing the estate of a deceased Meridian researcher containing a consciousness-anchoring methodology archive. MESA fast-closed the probate — likely to acquire the archive.
powers: Communication through formal letters — correct syntax, current knowledge, legally coherent
Obligation enforcement — can act on formal obligations through means that defy his deceased state
entities.harm: Indirect — through obligation-bound action at 14-day deadline
entities.weakness: Satisfying or formally dissolving the obligation in his own legal terms. He operates within his own logic — that logic can be used against him.
entities.bim_note: Deceased Meridian researcher's estate contained consciousness-anchoring methodology archive — directly relevant to Project Veil. MESA fast-closed the probate to acquire it.
keeper_moves: A new letter arrives — formal, precise, describing the next step
Wentworth's obligation-logic draws closer to Mira Okonkwo
A legal instrument Wentworth generated appears to have real force

entities.name: The Understudies
entities.keeper_description: Two lead actors (Clara Voss, Edmund Farrow) died 6 months apart. Three understudies are now hospitalised with complete identity loss. Four remaining understudies are at risk. The Saturday performance will trigger the next wave if not resolved. Grant source: Solstice Property Group — MESA shell company. Resolution requires a theatrical farewell within the logic of performance. Sven is the natural resolution vector — he exists between states, can enact the farewell the deceased leads need without personal cost.
powers: Identity displacement — proximity during performance overwrites the understudy's sense of self
Escalation on performance — each performance increases the displacement effect
entities.harm: Identity loss — not physical harm. Hospitalised understudies are alive but no longer themselves.
entities.weakness: Theatrical farewell within the logic of performance. The deceased leads need to perform their final scene. Sven can enact this without risk of identity displacement.
entities.bim_note: Grant source: Solstice Property Group (MESA shell company). MESA created the conditions. Probable BIM-class mechanism — performance as a liminal threshold.
keeper_moves: Another understudy shows early signs of identity displacement
The Saturday performance date becomes a hard deadline
MESA representative (not Rook) attends a rehearsal — observing, not intervening

entities.name: The Hollow
entities.keeper_description: Two individuals from the Hargrove Medical Centre Meridian trial experienced catastrophic identity erasure — the Hollow classification. MESA is monitoring their drift patterns. They are not contained. Available as a future threat. Their existence suggests the Meridian methodology has a failure mode worse than death.
powers: Complete identity erasure — selfhood lost, pattern of former consciousness displaced outward
Drift — not contained, moving through space in a pattern MESA is tracking
entities.harm: Unknown — classification based on two confirmed cases
entities.bim_note: Hargrove trial was the origin of Meridian BioSciences' BIM-adjacent methodology. These individuals are the worst-case outcome.
keeper_moves: MESA data stream references T-006 drift coordinates — hunters are not supposed to see this
A location shows signs of recent passage — no cognitive map residue at all, as if the space was never mapped
